content.full_text:
Farn Polrucel PnecrICES CoulttssloN
428 J Street . Suite 620 o Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(9t6) 322-5660 e Fax (916) 322-0886
November 26,2003
Alix A. Rosenthal
Meyers, Nave, Riback, Silver & Wilson
555 12*. Street, Suite 1500
Oakland, CA94607
Re: Your Request for"Advice
Our File No. A-03-257
Dear Ms. Rosenthal:
,/
This letter is in response to your letter dated luly 24,2003, which has been
construed by the Executive Director of the Fair Political Practices Commission as a
request for written advice regarding the conflict of interest code requirelxents of the
Political Reform Act (the "Act").I
QUESTIONS
l. If the Dougherty Regional Fire Authority ('?ire Authority'') ceases to exist,
how does it terminate its existing conflict of interest code requirement?
2. If the Dougherty Regional Fire Authority ("Fire Authority'') becomes
inoperable and nonfunctioning, may it obtain an exemption under regulation 18751 and
suspend or terminate its existing conflict of interest code?
CONCLUSIONS
1. When the Fire Authority ceases to exist, it may cancel its conflict of interest
code effective as of the date of the Fire Authority's dissolution. A letter indicating this
should be provided to the Commission, as the Fire Authority's code reviewing body. No
prior approval of the Commission is required.
I Government Code sections 81000 - gl}l4. Commission regulations appear at Title 2, sections
18109 - 18997, of the California Code of Regulations. : 
l
E3A'l


Dougherty Regional Fire Authority
Our File No. A-03-257
Page2
2. By separate letter on this date, the Commission's Executive Director
concluded that the exemption potentially available to an agency under regulation 18751
does not apply to agencies such as the Fire Authority which already have an approved
conflict of interest code in effect. Therefore, we offer no advice regarding this question.
FACTS
The Fire Authority is a multi-jurisdictional agency created under a joint power
agreement by the Cities of Dublin and San Ramon. The Fire Authority adopted a
conflict of interest code on October 13, 1988, under which certain positions were
designated and disclosure categories assigned. In 1997, the joint power agreement was
u*"rd"d to specify that the purpose of its board of directors was to wind up the affairs of
the Fire Authority and that it was to cease having any employees, effective July l,1997.
However, the Fire Authority continues to exist in order to continue distributing retirement
benefits to former employees. In April 2003, the positions of fire chief assistance fire
chief, and battalion chief were deleted from the list of employees designated in its
conflict of interest code; the potition of consultant was added to the list of designated
employees. The Fire Authority no longer provides fire-related services to the public.
ANALYSIS
/
1. Terminating an Existing Conflict of Interest Code
Government Code section 87300 provides that:
o.Every agency shall adopt and promulgate a Conflict of Interest Code
pursuant to the provisions of this article. A Conflict of Interest Code shall
-hur. th" fot"" of lu* and any violation of a Conflict of Interest Code by a
designated employee shall be deemed a violation of this chapter."
When complying with section 87300, any question as to whether a department
should be deemed an agency, for purposes of adopting and promulgating a conflict of
interest code, is to be resolved by the appropriate code reviewing body. (Section 87301.)
In addition, once an agency adopts a conflict of interest code, the code reviewing body's
approval is necessary before it may go into effect. (Section 87303.) Moreover, if an
ug.n"y fails to submit, adopt or amend a conflict of interest code within the time limits
piescribed under the Act, the code reviewing body may issue any appropriate order
directed to that agency, including adopting a conflict of interest code for that agency.
(Section 87304.) 
:
Although neither the Act nor the Commission's regulations explicitly address
requirements with respect to an agency's conflict of interest code when the agency ceases
to exist, it is implicit from the provisions above that when an agency ceases to exist,


Dougherty Regional Fire Authority
Our File No. A-03-257
Page 3
cancellation of its conflict of interest code is to be accompanied by reporting of the same
to its code reviewing body. The code reviewing body would then have discretion to
respond with a written acknowledgement that the agency's conflict of interest code has
been cancelled or with an appropriate order concerning the agency's code should, for
instance, it disagree with the agency's claim that it no longer exists.
2. Exemption under Regulation 18751
Regulation 18751 establishes procedures and standards for requesting and
receiving an exemption from the otherwise broad mandate of section 87300. You have
directed a requeit for exemption under regulation 18751 to the Commission's Executive
Director. In a separate letter provided this date, the Executive Director has denied your
request and construed it, in part, as a request for written advice, to which we have
responded above. Your question concerning the exemption under regulation 18751 is
fuliy addressed in the Executive Director's letter and we offer no advice concerning that
exemption.
If you have any other qhestions regarding his matter, please contact me at (916)
322-5660.
Sincerely,
Luisa Menchaca
Counsel
By:
L,
lick
Counsel, Legal Division
KLGjg
I\Adviceltrsl03-257

suspend or terminate its existing conflict of interest code?
